category_title: Visually impaired student with no speech
minimal_context: The student is using an iPad app and a communication book to make choices for a story in the classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's make a story using the iPad app. Look at the photos on the screen to choose our main character.

STUDENT: (Jane cannot see the screen because of her visual problems.)

TUTOR: I will give you some choices. Is the character a dog, a cat, or a bird?

STUDENT: (Jane uses her verbal communication book to indicate 'None of the above'.)

TUTOR: Okay, look closely at the iPad screen to see the other picture options.

STUDENT: (Jane is not able to use the photos or the iPad in the same way as the others due to her eyesight.)

options.A: Deliver brief, clear directives one at a time, providing sufficient wait time to comply.
options.B: Supplement the iPad story-making activity with an acted-out session for the student.
options.C: Immediately remove all demands and iPad task materials from the student for 60 seconds.
options.D: Turn toward the student, ask 'What do you need?', and provide brief physical contact.
